module_mood_contract: M5 mechanical rescue — 'Ahh, this is actually fixable.' Fabrication has signature shapes. The fix is mechanical: tighten the rules, regenerate, check again. Rescue not triumph: the judge is plain about its blind spots, M3's strategic uncertainty and M4's security residual still stand. Hand-off to M6 is hunger, not closure.
trace_status: refire_2026-05-02 (Debrief phase regenerated; all other phases SHA-stable from 48f2980)

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Meta-frame lectures are closers, not openers
rules_evaluated.evidence: The 'Grounded, and four candidates to measure' lecture precedes the exercise (opener — arms the student). The 'Self-consistency after the scoreboard' lecture follows the exercise (closer — names what they did). Both placements correct.

Pre-exercise content is two orientation paragraphs plus a lecture link. Does not pre-name practitioner terms or map frameworks with reasons. Opener arms; exercise delivers felt-evidence; self-consistency lecture is the closer.
Four-subagent benchmark: student initiates then watches. Detectors run in parallel, scorer adjudicates, student watches the scoreboard. Student does not paste every intermediate reply.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Trust the agentic design — no human backstops
rules_evaluated.evidence: No 'observe manually' or exhaustive if-then rulesets. Scorer picks the winner; student watches. Debrief instructs student to push back on Claude's summary, not audit manually. Agent Actions section uses 'propose, double-check, apply' — agent-first.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Exercise quality ladder — target 8, design 9 for peaks
rules_evaluated.evidence: M5 is rescue-mood arc — design-9 target. Hallucination benchmark is the magic beat: understandable mechanism, re-runnable artifact, traveling vocabulary (scoreboard, precision/recall, Known limit), WoW moment (scoreboard filling). Maintainer block confirms Understandable magic bar met.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Teach the principle and the technique — default
rules_evaluated.evidence: Key Concepts section explains WHY empirical selection works, HOW the scoreboard produces the winner, WHAT the judge's Known limit means. Maintainer block names Understandable magic bar explicitly. Black-box moves absent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Forcing functions in prompts not body prose
rules_evaluated.evidence: REFIRE CLEARED. Prior REVISE: pattern-label 'Name the boundary, then check the work' lived only in body prose after the fenced Debrief prompt — structurally unreachable for speed-runners. Fix applied: the Debrief prompt's closing instruction now reads 'Then name the two patterns this prompt enforced: one about which boundary you were allowed to touch, one about checking what landed after you wrote it.' This instruction is inside the fenced block. Speed-runners who paste-and-run will get Claude to articulate both patterns by name in its response. The body callout ('Notice what this prompt insists on...') is retained for engaged readers — per rule 16 carve-out, optional-refinement context can stay loose in body. Mandatory teaching beat is now fenced. Diagnostic passes: reading prompt-to-prompt only, the two-pattern transfer lands via Claude's own mouth at run time.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Measure silent failure, not just friction
rules_evaluated.evidence: Close's still-uncertain carry-forward and Known limit line in judge file design for productive incompleteness. Debrief's 'LEAST confident' question is a designed gap-finder.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Between-module reading appears in two places
rules_evaluated.evidence: This file correctly places 'Read Ethan Mollick, Garbage Can and Bitter Lesson' in the Homework after Module 5 section. Second placement in M6's Prework section cannot be verified from this file alone. Non-blocking todo carried forward.
rules_evaluated.fix_hint: Verify curriculum/trainings/bootstrap/evaluations.md Prework section includes 'Read Ethan Mollick, Garbage Can and Bitter Lesson.' If missing, add it.

rules_evaluated.evidence: Maintainer block explicitly names: 'Mood contract — mechanical rescue, not triumph.' Student-facing prose honours the contract: 'This module is the rescue. Not full closure.' Key Concepts last bullet names what the judge can't reach. Next paragraph plants the 300-claims hunger for M6.

Unease is 'I'd like to know which claims are grounded and I couldn't tell before' — not 'I failed to check my work.' Rescue framing attributes problem to generation mechanics, not student error. Known limit line prevents triumphant close.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Lead with the discipline, not the failure mode
rules_evaluated.evidence: Big Idea leads with the discipline ('you run several on your own output, score them against a 30-claim benchmark, and keep the winner') not with 'hallucination.' Key Concepts lead with 'Benchmarking as a pattern,' 'Empirical method selection over authority,' 'A judge is narrow on purpose' — all positive disciplines.

Module correctly sits at M5. Benchmark uses M3's synthesized briefing — cross-module artefact contract explicit in Start Here prose. Judge file saves to judges/groundedness-judge.md — M6 picks this up. Module does not front-run M6's eval loop. Four detectors internally consistent.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Strategic themes recur in Key Concepts
rules_evaluated.evidence: Bootstrap themes visible in Key Concepts: 'Benchmarking as a pattern' (practice beats external proof), 'Empirical method selection over authority' (practice beats authority), 'The scoreboard is the artifact' (verification is the work), 'A judge is narrow on purpose' (narrow tools that work beat broad tools that pretend), 'Grounded is the discipline' (discipline framing). Three or more theme-instances present.
Debrief prompt contains no [BRACKET] placeholders. All references are deterministic paths ('./CLAUDE.md', 'module-5/evidence-roster.md', 'judges/groundedness-judge.md'). No student fill-in required.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Every prompt block gets a one-sentence action lead-in
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt preceded by: 'Ask Claude to review the session and update ./CLAUDE.md with groundedness operating rules.' Command verb, under 20 words, semantically accurate.
Debrief prompt uses **Prompt** *(Claude Code)* chip, blank line, fenced block. No copy prefix, no end-of-prompt closer, no blockquote wrapping.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Chain prompts by back-reference and deterministic path
rules_evaluated.evidence: Debrief prompt names deterministic paths for all five input files and the output section. Back-reference via session context ('Review this session'). No invented aliases.

rules_evaluated.rule_lead: Markdown shout inside fenced prompt blocks is dead weight
rules_evaluated.evidence: No bold or italic markdown inside the fenced Debrief prompt. Emphasis through sentence structure and plain prose.

phase_scores.note: Good arrival state — cases prime productive unease. Reused from prior trace (SHA-stable).
Rescue beginning to materialise; oriented and ready. Reused from prior trace (SHA-stable).
Scoreboard magic moment — 'ahh this is fixable' fully felt. Reused from prior trace (SHA-stable).
Extends rescue without implying incompleteness. Reused from prior trace (SHA-stable).

REGENERATED. Prompt now closes: 'Then name the two patterns this prompt enforced: one about which boundary you were allowed to touch, one about checking what landed after you wrote it.' Persona pastes the extended Debrief prompt. Claude reads ./CLAUDE.md, scans five module-5/ files and judges/groundedness-judge.md, proposes 1-4 groundedness rules, adds them to the Groundedness checks section, reads back the section verbatim, gives a 4-line summary, then names the two patterns. The two-pattern naming is Claude's own closing move — it reads as completing the rescue report, not as a new student obligation. Mood stays mechanical-rescue: the student is listening to Claude account for what it just enforced. Body callout ('Notice what this prompt insists on...') follows as recognition text for engaged readers — reads as 'here's what just happened,' not 'here's another thing to do.' No shift to vigilance. Still-uncertain carry-forward and Known limit line in the judge file keep the close from tipping to triumph.
Extends rescue to operational practice calmly. Reused from prior trace (SHA-stable).
Hunger not closure — M6 hook well-planted. Reused from prior trace (SHA-stable).

stealing_the_mood.location: Debrief prompt closing instruction — two-pattern naming
stealing_the_mood.risk: VERY LOW. The new closing instruction ('Then name the two patterns this prompt enforced: one about which boundary you were allowed to touch, one about checking what landed after you wrote it.') asks Claude to name patterns IT enforced, not the student to perform a new task. The framing is retrospective-report, not forward-obligation. For the SVP persona running self-study, this reads as: Claude wraps its report by naming the mechanic — same genre as 'and that's why source-triangulation won.' No shift to vigilance. For speed-runners specifically: they paste and run, Claude gives groundedness rules + confirmation + two named patterns. The speed-runner gets the transfer teaching from Claude's mouth without reading the body callout at all. This is exactly the forcing-function intent.
stealing_the_mood.assessment: Mood contract intact. Rule 16 cleared. The prompt extension completes the rescue, not extends the vigilance obligation.

todos.description: Homework 'Carry your own module-5/still-uncertain.md line into Module 6 prework' references a filename (module-5/still-uncertain.md) that the Debrief prompt does not create. The still-uncertain content lands in ./CLAUDE.md (as the LEAST-confident answer Claude gave in the Debrief summary). Either (a) update the homework line to reference where the content actually lives, or (b) add a step in the Debrief that saves the LEAST-confident line to module-5/still-uncertain.md explicitly.

Rule 28 cross-file check: verify that evaluations.md Prework section includes 'Read Ethan Mollick, Garbage Can and Bitter Lesson.' If missing, add it. Cannot be confirmed from output-quality.md alone.
